mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a regression coefficient on the interaction term statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in over 60% of observations during tightening cycles, invalidating the supply-demand transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, boosting corporate revenue forecasts and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly increases aggregate corporate earnings and economic activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the predictive slope coefficient is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
